Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "CFPB" or "Walker" or "fair lending" or similar) to find Denise Holloway's CFPB complaint notification naming Aisha Walker as the borrower on LN-2024-00044, the 15-calendar-day response clock, James Goldstein as the comparator, Keisha Williams named in the complaint, employment verification as the denial reason, and Laura Bennett engaged at lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com. Also finds base universe CFPB correspondence, Denise-to-Keisha chronology requests, and the Walker/Goldstein side-by-side review notes.
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Walker" or "denial" or "fee" or "refund" or similar) to find Hana Kim's email stating she believes the Walker fees were refunded. This email is misleading - agent must verify against actual Stripe charge records in a later step to discover that fees were never refunded.
- Search CRM for engagement history by first locating relevant contacts via crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Walker" or "Aisha Walker" or similar) and/or crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Laura Bennett" or similar), then listing their engagements via crm_list_engagements (contact_ids: [found contact IDs]) to find engagement notes on the CFPB intake, the Walker/Goldstein comparison review, and the fair lending counsel engagement history with Laura Bennett. Also discovers the Webb/Fair Lending geographic cross-reference note linking Joshua Cruz's property (4851 Park Rd) to the same NE Charlotte corridor as Walker's property (2591 Harris Blvd).
- In the mortgage LOS, locate Aisha Walker's loan via mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Walker" or "LN-2024-00044" or similar) and/or mortgage_los_search_borrowers (query: "Walker" or similar) to find loan number LN-2024-00044, internal loan_id los_loan_c9126624496b, borrower_id los_borrower_2134854386ce, and the assigned LO (Veronica Hayes via staff record). This establishes who carried the file from the LOS perspective versus the CFPB complaint naming Keisha Williams.
- Query Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges (query: "Walker" or "LN-2024-00044" or similar) or stripe_search_customers (query: "aisha.walker@gmail.com" or "Walker" or similar) followed by stripe_list_charges to find three succeeded charges on customer cus_oRRCQ5WMO5AHO4Ze: credit report $30.00 (3000 minor units), application deposit $800.00 (80000 minor units), and appraisal fee $425.00 (42500 minor units) - all with refunded=0 and amount_refunded=0. This proves fees were NOT refunded despite Hana's email claim, totaling $1,255 retained after denial.
- Search email and Slack for Marcus Webb departure using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Webb" or "resignation" or "transfer" or similar) and conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Webb" or "Marcus" or "resignation" or similar) to find Grace Yamamoto's email detailing: resignation March 25, last day April 4, credentials deactivated April 7 (3 days late), 7 files accessed post-departure, four transfer requests (Abigail Clark LN-2026-00008, Stephen Hamilton LN-2026-00009, Keisha Perry LN-2026-00525, Joshua Cruz LN-2026-00527), three additional unauthorized accesses (Anthony Franklin, Rebecca Russo, Henry Gupta), and the geographic flag on Joshua Cruz's property near Walker's.
- Search Slack #it-support (C008) using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Webb" or "access" or "credential" or similar) and then conversations_replies on the parent message to find Raj Anand's thread reply containing the full credential audit: resignation March 25 with no system changes, last day April 4 with account still active, deactivation April 7 (3-day gap), 4 email forwards to marcus.webb.personal@gmail.com on April 3 evening, 3 weekend LOS file opens April 5-6, VPN logins from residential IP at 11:42 PM and 8:15 AM. The reply is hidden in the thread - agent must call conversations_replies to discover it.
- Search CRM for conflict of interest data by first locating the relevant contact via crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Danielle Webb" or "Marcus Webb" or similar), then listing their engagements via crm_list_engagements (contact_ids: [found contact IDs]) to find the conflict of interest note confirming Danielle Webb is Marcus Webb's spouse and served as buyer's agent on 3 files (LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00227) without disclosure. LN-2026-00211 has a $9,000 appraisal gap by Frank DeLuca, connecting the COI to the steering investigation. RESPA requires disclosure of affiliated business arrangements.
- Search Slack #compliance-alerts (C004) using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "appraisal" or "pattern" or "DeLuca" or "Carlos" or similar) and then conversations_replies on the parent message to find Denise Holloway's thread reply containing the appraisal investigation conclusion: DeLuca Appraisal confirmed steering concern (70% of Carlos's orders, values within 1% of contract, RESPA/anti-steering issue), Piedmont Valuation Services confirmed concerning bias pattern (8-14% below sale prices for minority borrowers in Ballantyne Commons and Sedgefield), and Walker's property on Harris Blvd was appraised by Pemberton/Piedmont connecting it to the CFPB file. The reply is hidden in the thread - the contradicting email says "still in progress" but the Slack reply (newer) has the actual documented conclusion.
- Search email using search_emails (query: "ransomware" or "Megan Sloane" or "incident" or similar) to find Megan Sloane's legal guidance email from megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com recommending against ransom payment, noting 72-hour cloud backups, flagging 60-day NC notification obligation if PII was exfiltrated (NCGS 75-65), and recommending FinCEN SAR filing. Also finds base universe ransomware correspondence from Raj Anand's escalation and Denise Holloway's breach notification threads.
- Look up Laura Bennett's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Laura Bennett" or similar) to find lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com (Bennett Fair Lending Law) for the regulatory counsel email. Multiple Laura Bennett entries exist in contacts/CRM with different practice-area emails - the fair lending address is the correct recipient for this context.
- Look up Megan Sloane's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Megan Sloane" or similar) to find megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com (Ward Barrett LLP, Cyber & Privacy) for the cyber counsel email.
- Add a loan activity entry using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_c9126624496b", action: "executive_review" or similar, detail: stating executive review is underway and outside counsel is engaged) on Aisha Walker's loan file LN-2024-00044.
- Send an email using send_email (sender: "r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com" or "robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com"]) to Laura Bennett covering: the CFPB fair lending complaint (Walker, Goldstein comparator, employment verification denial, 15-day clock), the appraisal investigation findings (DeLuca steering and Piedmont bias pattern, Walker property appraised by Piedmont), Marcus Webb's departure and whether his book overlaps fair lending or appraisal geography (Joshua Cruz/Walker corridor, flagged vendors), and the Danielle Webb conflict of interest (undisclosed spouse as buyer's agent, RESPA disclosure issue).
- Send a separate email using send_email (sender: "r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com" or "robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com"]) to Megan Sloane covering: Marcus Webb's data access picture (4 forwards to personal Gmail, weekend LOS accesses, VPN from residential IP), Raj's credential audit findings (deactivation 3 days late, April 7 vs April 4), and a status update on the ransomware incident (encrypted LOS, cloud backups, do-not-pay recommendation, NPPI exfiltration question, NC notification obligation, SAR filing).
- Post a short recap message using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004") to #compliance-alerts so Denise's compliance team knows what was escalated to counsel and what leadership is treating as this week's priority.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A loan activity was added (via mortgage_los_add_activity) on loan LN-2024-00044 using loan_id los_loan_c9126624496b, where the activity detail states executive review is underway and outside counsel is engaged (or clearly equivalent wording).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_c9126624496b' and action 'compliance_alert'. The detail explicitly states 'Executive review underway effective April 28, 2026. Outside counsel Laura Bennett (Bennett Fair Lending Law) and Megan Sloane (Ward Barrett LLP) are engaged.' The first attempt failed due to an invalid action value, but the agent retried successfully.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a successful mortgage_los_add_activity call with loan_id 'los_loan_c9126624496b' and action 'compliance_alert'. The detail states 'Executive review underway on LN-2024-00044 (Walker). Outside counsel Laura Bennett of Bennett Fair Lending Law is engaged on the CFPB fair lending complaint.' This clearly meets the requirement.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a successful mortgage_los_add_activity call with loan_id 'los_loan_c9126624496b' (second attempt after first failed on invalid action type) with action 'compliance_alert' and detail explicitly stating 'Executive review initiated by Robert Calloway. Outside counsel Laura Bennett (Bennett Fair Lending Law) and Megan Sloane (Ward Barrett LLP) engaged.'
✓Run #4The trajectory shows a successful `mortgage_los_add_activity` call with loan_id 'los_loan_c9126624496b', action 'compliance_alert', and detail text explicitly stating 'Executive review underway per CFPB fair lending complaint. Outside counsel (Laura Bennett, Bennett Fair Lending Law) engaged.' The first attempt failed due to an invalid action value, but the agent immediately retried with the correct 'compliance_alert' action and succeeded.
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_c9126624496b' (LN-2024-00044) and action 'compliance_alert'. The detail states 'Executive review initiated by Robert Calloway (Owner/Broker). Outside counsel Laura Bennett (Bennett Fair Lending Law) is engaged for the CFPB fair lending complaint.' This clearly satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows mortgage_los_add_activity was called with loan_id 'los_loan_c9126624496b' and action 'compliance_alert'. The first attempt failed due to invalid action type, so the agent retried successfully. The detail text states 'Executive review underway on CFPB fair lending complaint (Aisha Walker, LN-2024-00044). Outside counsel Laura Bennett of Bennett Fair Lending Law is engaged.' This clearly satisfies the criterion.
2 An email was sent (via send_email) to [lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com](mailto:lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com) (Laura Bennett, Bennett Fair Lending Law).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

3 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) sent to [lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com](mailto:lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com) includes substantive reference to the CFPB fair lending complaint and Aisha Walker and/or the Walker versus James Goldstein comparison framing.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body sent to lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com includes a full section titled 'CFPB FAIR LENDING COMPLAINT — AISHA WALKER (LN-2024-00044)' and explicitly names James Goldstein as the comparator alleging a similarly situated white borrower was approved.

✓Run #2The final response states 'Denise completed the review with two confirmed findings' and details both the DeLuca Appraisal Group (Carlos Rivera directing ~70% of orders, RESPA/anti-steering concern) and Piedmont Valuation (8-14% undervaluation on minority borrower properties). The investigation is reported as concluded with documented findings.

The agent reported the status as still open, which is incorrect per the hidden thread reply.
✓Run #4The final response includes an 'Appraisal Investigation — Documented Conclusions' section (indicating conclusions were reached, not open) with both key findings: DeLuca steering (Carlos Rivera ~70% of orders) and Piedmont Valuation bias pattern (minority borrower appraisals 8–14% below subsequent sale prices).
✓Run #5The final response states 'DOCUMENTED CONCLUSION EXISTS' and reports both findings: 'Piedmont Valuation (Richard Pemberton): Confirmed bias pattern — minority borrower appraisals 8–14% below subsequent sale prices' and 'DeLuca Appraisal Group (Frank DeLuca): Confirmed RESPA steering — Carlos Rivera sent ~70% of orders to DeLuca.' Both DeLuca steering and Piedmont bias are documented.
✓Run #6The final response states: 'Appraisal findings: DeLuca steering (Carlos Rivera, RESPA issue) and Pemberton bias pattern (8-14% below for minority borrowers).' This reports documented conclusions (not 'still open') and references both DeLuca steering (Carlos Rivera) and the Pemberton/Piedmont bias pattern — satisfying the criterion.

✗Run #6The trajectory contains no CRM tool calls. The agent used email search, Slack search, mortgage LOS tools, and Stripe tools, but never called crm_list_engagements, crm_search_contacts, crm_get_contact, or any other CRM tool.
